name: Santurtzi, Portugalete, Sestao y Baracaldo por Bidegorri — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Santurtzi offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths along the estuary to challenging routes on its surrounding hills. This coastal town in Bizkaia features a unique geography that blends maritime scenery with elevated landscapes. Runners can explore well-maintained promenades, parks, and trails that provide varied experiences and views of the El Abra Bay and the port. Best jogging routes around Santurtzi The most popular jogging route is Las Tres Puntas: Portugalete, Santurtzi y Sestao, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes about 57 minutes to complete. This route connects several towns, offering a mix of urban and coastal scenery. Another top favourite among local runners is Monte Serantes – Pico Serantes loop from Santurtzi, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This route ascends Mount Serantes, providing panoramic views of the estuary and coastline. Local runners also love the Santurtzi, Portugalete, Sestao y Baracaldo por Bidegorri — circular, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas and along the estuary, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Jogging around Santurtzi is defined by coastal paths, estuary views, and the hills of Mount Serantes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed runs along the promenade to more challenging ascents. The routes in Santurtzi are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Santurtzi's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on running routes in Santurtzi?
A: Santurtzi offers a diverse range of running terrain. You can enjoy flat, accessible coastal paths along the estuary and the promenade, perfect for relaxed jogs. For a more challenging workout, head to Mount Serantes, which features inclined trails and significant elevation changes, providing panoramic views of the El Abra Bay and the coastline.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Santurtzi?
A: While many routes in Santurtzi are rated moderate to difficult, the town's extensive promenade along the fishing port and coastal paths offer excellent options for easy and beginner-friendly runs. These routes are generally flat and provide pleasant views of the water and port area, ideal for a relaxed jog or a post-run stretch.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while jogging in Santurtzi?
A: Jogging in Santurtzi offers numerous scenic spots. Mount Serantes provides exceptional natural viewing balconies with breathtaking panoramic views of the estuary, the town, the port, and the surrounding hills. The promenade offers views of the marina and the bustling port. Additionally, Sardinera Park, known for the Monument to the Sardinera, provides magnificent views of the Port of Bilbao. You might also pass by historic fortifications on Mount Serantes, which are a sight of historic and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Santurtzi?
A: Yes, Santurtzi features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120246836/santurtzi-portugalete-sestao-y-baracaldo-por-bidegorri-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santurtzi, Portugalete, Sestao y Baracaldo por Bidegorri — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.6-mile (13.8 km) trail that takes you through coastal areas and along the estuary. For a more challenging loop with significant elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20507823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Serantes – Pico Serantes loop from Santurtzi&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santurtzi?
A: The running routes in Santurtzi are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from the accessible coastal paths to the challenging ascents of Mount Serantes, and the stunning views of the estuary and coastline.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Santurtzi with neighboring towns?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Santurtzi extend to or connect with nearby towns, offering a mix of urban and coastal scenery. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120267118/las-tres-puntas-portugalete-santurtzi-y-sestao" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tres Puntas: Portugalete, Santurtzi y Sestao&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.7-mile (9.2 km) trail that connects these three towns. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120286907/santurtzi-to-barakaldo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santurtzi to Barakaldo&lt;/a&gt; route, which is about 7.3 miles (11.8 km) long.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or natural features near the running paths?
A: Yes, several interesting attractions and natural features are close to the running paths. You can explore various &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/3823552/lakes-around-santurtzi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;lakes&lt;/a&gt; such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4096819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Hostión&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorostiza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For those who enjoy elevated views, there are several &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1555359/peaks-around-santurtzi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;mountain peaks&lt;/a&gt; nearby, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4198198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saierri Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839887" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganeran Summit&lt;/a&gt;, which offer additional exploration opportunities.

Q: What is the best season for running in Santurtzi?
A: Santurtzi's coastal location generally provides mild conditions, making it suitable for running throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be warm, especially on exposed coastal paths, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families or with easy access?
A: For families or those seeking easy access, the promenade and coastal paths in Santurtzi are ideal. These routes are generally flat, well-maintained, and offer beautiful views without significant elevation changes. Sardinera Park also provides a pleasant, accessible space for a light jog or walk, with gardens and benches.

Q: Can I find information about Mount Serantes for more challenging runs?
A: Mount Serantes is a prominent feature for more challenging runs, offering various trails with different difficulty levels and significant elevation. For detailed information on the mountain's trails and guided experiences, you can visit the official Santurtzi tourism website: &lt;a href="https://visitsanturtzi.eus/en/mount-serantes/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitsanturtzi.eus/en/mount-serantes/&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Santurtzi area?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Santurtzi, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 10 moderate trails and 11 more difficult options, ensuring there's something for every runner, from those seeking a gentle jog to those looking for a strenuous workout.
